title: Download FREE Music for YouTube (No Copyright)
description:
There’s a free YouTube music library hiding inside YouTube Studio that most creators never use. In this quick tutorial, I’ll show you exactly where to find copyright‑safe, royalty‑free music for your YouTube videos so you can stop worrying about takedowns and copyright claims. You’ll learn how to use YouTube’s Creator Music “Free” tab to grab background tracks that are cleared for monetization on your channel.

To get there, open YouTube Studio, then on the left click Creator Music. At the top, switch to the Free tab and hit “View all” so you only see tracks that cost zero, no subscription or external site needed. This is YouTube’s built‑in audio library: a catalog of no‑copyright and royalty‑free tracks you’re allowed to use in your YouTube videos without risking strikes, claims, or lost ad revenue.
​

From there, use the filters for genre, mood, duration, and more to quickly find music that fits the vibe of your vlog, tutorial, or YouTube Shorts. There are tons of free background music options to choose from, and some tracks may ask for simple attribution in your description, so always double‑check the usage notes. If this helped you find safe music for your next upload, subscribe for more practical YouTube creator tips.
